title: Local API server to trigger Claude story creation and PR publishing
user_story: As a developer, I want to trigger Claude to create a user story or publish a pull request via a local API call, so that I can integrate these workflows into my own scripts and tooling without invoking the Claude Code CLI directly.
rationale: The team already has story-publisher and pr-publisher skills backed by automation scripts. Exposing them through a lightweight local HTTP server lets developers curl an endpoint or call it from any script, making Claude-driven story and PR creation composable with other tooling without requiring interactive CLI use.
machine_acceptance_criteria: Given the local server is running, when a POST /story request is made with a JSON body containing a 'prompt' field, then the server invokes the story-publisher workflow and returns the created issue URL in the response.

Given the local server is running, when a POST /pr request is made with a JSON body containing an optional 'branch' field, then the server invokes the pr-publisher workflow and returns the created PR URL in the response.
Given a request is missing required fields, when the server processes it, then it returns a 400 response with a descriptive error message.
Given ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set in the environment, when the server starts, then it exits with a clear error message rather than silently failing at request time.
Given a workflow invocation fails, when the server handles the error, then it returns a 500 response with the error output rather than hanging.
human_acceptance_criteria: Run the server locally and curl POST /story with a prompt; verify a GitHub issue is created and the URL is returned in the response.
Run the server locally and curl POST /pr; verify a pull request is opened and the PR URL is returned.
Verify a missing 'prompt' field returns a 400 with a clear message.
Verify startup fails with a readable error if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set.
implementation_plan: 1. Create automation/server.ts (or server.js): a minimal HTTP server (Node built-in or Express) with POST /story and POST /pr routes.
2. POST /story: validate 'prompt' field, shell out to the existing publish_story.sh script (or Claude Code SDK call) with the prompt, stream stdout/stderr, return { issueUrl } on success.
3. POST /pr: validate optional 'branch' field, shell out to the publish_pr.sh script, return { prUrl } on success.
4. Add startup check for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GH_TOKEN environment variables; exit(1) with a message if missing.
5. Add a start script to package.json: 'automation:server': 'ts-node automation/server.ts' (or node equivalent).
6. Document usage with example curl commands in README under a 'Local Automation API' section.
backend_changes: New automation/server.ts implementing the local HTTP API.
Reuse existing automation/scripts/publish_story.sh and automation/scripts/publish_pr.sh as the execution layer.
test_changes: Unit tests for request validation: missing 'prompt' → 400; valid body → calls the correct script.
Integration smoke test: start the server, POST /story with a mock prompt, assert the script is invoked with expected args.
known_risks_assumptions: The server runs locally only and is not intended for deployment; no auth is added in this story.
Assumes the existing shell scripts are correct and stable; errors in those scripts surface as 500 responses.
Shell-out latency depends on Claude API response time; the HTTP response will block until the workflow completes.
No concurrency handling in v1 — simultaneous requests may conflict on automation/out/story.json; follow-up story can add request queuing.
